title: ACES Scenario Pack — Provenance (v1)
description: The four-part provenance ledger: a first-class sources ledger, a per-artifact-root distribution class, an all-true content-safety attestation, and a per-gate publication-review checklist. Records references only: it must not copy private source content, real credentials, operator tokens, private hosts, or customer data. Distribution class (what may be published or sold) is a separate axis from runtime visibility (who may see an artifact root at runtime). The content-safety all-true policy, blocked-review rejection, attribution completeness, source-reference integrity, and consumer-overlay path containment / non-overlap are enforced by the provenance validation gate, which the schema alone cannot express.

packId.description: Pack this provenance record applies to.

sources.description: First-class ledger of where the pack's content originated, expressed as references. Covers upstream corpora, frameworks, tools, datasets, research, original design, and generated material.

id.description: Stable source id, referenced by artifact roots.

kind.description: What kind of source this is.

license.description: License governing the source (an identifier or short name), never copied license text of private material.

usage.description: Usage terms: how this source may be used in the pack.

attributionRequired.description: Whether attribution is required when this source is used.

attributionText.description: Attribution to render when attributionRequired is true. The provenance gate requires this to be present and non-empty in that case.

used.description: What was used from this source.

excluded.description: What was deliberately excluded from this source.

ref.description: A non-sensitive reference (path or identifier), never copied private content.

note.description: Short adoption note about this source.

scrubStatus.description: Whether downstream-private names, paths, labels, statuses, and assumptions have been scrubbed. Orthogonal to the content-safety attestation and to publication review.

ownership.description: Which boundary owns the content (ACES core, this repository, or a consumer).

artifacts.description: Per-artifact-root distribution class: the 'what may be published, redistributed, or sold' axis. Separate from runtime visibility, artifact-boundary disposition, lifecycle state, and publication-review status.

path.description: Pack-relative artifact root (a file or directory subtree). A contract mapping, not a required directory name.

distributionClass.description: What may be done with this root's content when distributing the pack. 'consumer-specific' declares a removable overlay root; the provenance gate requires overlay roots to be path-contained and to never overlap a base (non-consumer-specific) root.

sources.description: Source ids (from sources[].id) this root's content derives from. The provenance gate flags any id that does not resolve to a declared source.

note.description: Short note about this root's distribution class.

contentSafety.description: Content-safety attestation modelled as named boolean gates. The provenance gate requires every gate to be true for a releasable pack; the policy is exclusion of real sensitive content, never a weaker class.

noRealThirdPartyTargets.description: No real third-party targets are included.

noSensitiveData.description: No real sensitive data is included.

offensiveToolingBoundary.description: Offensive-tooling content stays within the declared boundary (no real weaponized capability).

publicationReview.description: Per-gate publication-review checklist. Review status describes clearance to publish, not whether the content is safe. The provenance gate rejects any 'blocked' gate; 'pending' and 'approved' are allowed.

consumerOverlay.description: Optional consumer-overlay review status, present when overlays exist.
